sections.paragraph:
sometimes עֹוז ( Pro 31:17, 25 Pro 31:25); followed by Makk. עָז־, with suff. עֻזִּי and עָזִּי, עֻזִּךָ and עָזְּךָ, עֻזֹּו, עוּזֵּנוּ (from the root עָזַז)-

(1) strength, might, power, used of God, Job 12:16 26:2 of men, Psa 29:11 Pro 24:5 of beasts, Job 41:14 of a loud voice, Psa 68:34 of vehemence of anger, Psa 90:11. בְּכָל־עֹז with all (one’s) might, 2Sa 6:14. Concr. the strong, heroes, Jud. 5:21 .

(2) firmness, מִגְדַּל עֹז a firm, secure, fortified tower, Jud. 9:51 comp. Psa 30:8. Hence trop. defence, refuge, protection. Psa 28:8, יְהֹוָה עֹז לָמֹו “Jehovah (is a) protection for them.” Psa 46:2 62:8 . In a bad sense עֹז פָּנִים strength of countenance, i.e. impudence, Ecc 8:1.-With the idea of power are joined those of majesty, splendour, glory. Hence it is-

(3) splendour, majesty, i.q. כָּבֹוד with which it is often joined, Hab 3:4. Psa 96:6, עֹז וְתִפְאֶרֶת “splendour and majesty.” Psa 132:8, אֲרֹון עֻזֶּךָ “the ark (the seat) of thy majesty,” i.e. the ark of the covenant, (elsewhere אֲרֹון כְּבֹוד יְהֹוָה 2Ch 6:41); called poet. עֹז alone, Psa 78:61 compare 1Sa 4:21, 22 1Sa 4:22.

(4) glory, praise, Psa 8:3 29:1 68:35 99:4 Exo 15:2. 2Ch 30:21, כְּלֵי־עֹז “instruments of praise,” employed in praising God. (Arab. عِزُّ power, victory, glory.)
